page: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring dedicated hardware resources. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 36 physical cores and 72 vCPUs at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It features a high-speed network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c5n.metal demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 67384.33 and a PassMark CPU score of 50868.09, both placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also highly competitive, with read-only memory bandwidth reaching 3741.32 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 212.97 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1193.0. While LLM prompt processing speeds are average, text generation performance is poor, with the 7B model generating only 6.09 tokens per second, highlighting a limitation for real-time generative AI workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5n.metal offers high resource density and direct bare-metal access to CPU and memory resources, eliminating virtualization overhead. This makes it highly efficient for workloads that require high-throughput networking and intensive parallel computation. The 100 Gbps network bandwidth, combined with strong multi-core and database operation benchmarks, makes this instance well-suited for high-performance computing, network appliances, distributed data processing, and intensive database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 36 physical cores and 72 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory read bandwidth, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to poor. This instance is highly efficient for workloads requiring direct hardware access and high network throughput, making it suitable for high-performance computing, network appliances, and distributed database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 72 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput parallel workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed 100 Gbps networking and dedicated multi-core Intel Xeon processing for high-throughput workloads.

192.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
Strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth benchmark performance
Best suited for high-performance computing, network appliances, and database operations
